members.name: AutoExposureEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables the tonemapping auto exposure mode of the scene renderer. If [code]true[/code], the renderer will automatically determine the exposure setting to adapt to the scene's illumination and the observed light. [b]Note:[/b] Auto-exposure is only supported in the Forward+ rendering method, not Mobile or Compatibility.

members.name: AutoExposureScale
members.summary: The scale of the auto exposure effect. Affects the intensity of auto exposure.

members.name: AutoExposureSpeed
members.summary: The speed of the auto exposure effect. Affects the time needed for the camera to perform auto exposure.

members.name: ExposureMultiplier
members.summary: Multiplier for the exposure amount. A higher value results in a brighter image.

members.name: ExposureSensitivity
members.summary: Sensitivity of camera sensors, measured in ISO. A higher sensitivity results in a brighter image. If [member auto_exposure_enabled] is [code]true[/code], this can be used as a method of exposure compensation, doubling the value will increase the exposure value (measured in EV100) by 1 stop. [b]Note:[/b] Only available when [member ProjectSettings.rendering/lights_and_shadows/use_physical_light_units] is enabled.
